raw_response:
## Findings — CC6.6 Boundary Logical Access Security / Service Mesh mTLS

| # | Finding | Related Document Section | Why It’s a Gap | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **`schema-registry` is not enforcing mTLS** | **Section 3.1 — mTLS Requirement**; **Section 3.3 — Exceptions** | The policy requires all production service-to-service communication to use mTLS with Istio `PeerAuthentication` set to **STRICT**, with no plaintext fallback. The TLS audit shows `schema-registry` has sidecar injection enabled but `PeerAuthentication` is set to **PERMISSIVE**, meaning it accepts both mTLS and plaintext traffic. This does not meet the stated requirement that services without valid client certificates are rejected at the mesh layer. The audit notes a compatibility issue with a legacy Kafka Connect plugin, but no formal GRC exception, compensating controls, time-bound remediation plan, or CISO approval was provided. | **High** |
| 2 | **`metrics-exporter` is outside the Istio service mesh and does not use mTLS** | **Section 3.1 — mTLS Requirement**; **Section 2 — Scope** | The policy applies to all production microservices in the EKS clusters and requires every production service to have an Istio sidecar proxy injected. The TLS audit shows `metrics-exporter` has **no Istio sidecar injected**, `PeerAuthentication` is **N/A**, and there is **no mutual authentication**. Because it exports Prometheus metrics from all namespaces, this service has broad visibility across the production environment and represents a boundary-control gap if accessed by unauthorized internal or external-originated traffic. | **High** |
| 3 | **`metrics-exporter` uses non-standard, self-signed TLS instead of Vault-issued service certificates** | **Section 3.2 — Certificate Management** | The policy requires all service certificates to be issued by the HashiCorp Vault PKI engine using the approved certificate chain and rotated automatically every 72 hours. The audit shows `metrics-exporter` uses a **self-signed TLS certificate generated by a Helm chart**, TLS 1.2, and has no recorded certificate rotation. This bypasses the approved certificate issuance, trust, revocation, and rotation controls. | **High** |
| 4 | **Formal exception evidence is missing for non-compliant production services** | **Section 3.3 — Exceptions** | The policy allows services that cannot support mTLS only if they obtain a formal exception with business justification, compensating controls, a time-bound remediation plan, and CISO approval. The audit identifies two non-compliant services: `schema-registry` and `metrics-exporter`. The report includes operational explanations, but no approved exceptions or remediation timelines were provided. | **Medium** |
